summary: First-iteration end-to-end testing of NEXUS ARENA. 22/22 backend pytest tests passed. Frontend flows all functional: home, marketplace, game pages, admin login, dashboard, post creation, marketplace listing, account detail. One minor issue: the /admin25 AUTHENTICATE button submit click occasionally does not fire the POST /api/auth/login request (works reliably by pressing Enter). Likely custom-cursor overlay interception. All other click actions worked with force=True.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking [data-testid="admin-login-submit"] with force=True does not always trigger the login network request (first attempt no request was dispatched; loading state briefly toggled). Enter-key submit works consistently. Likely the custom cursor overlay (cursor:none + big cursor div with pointer-events) intercepts the pointerdown on centered buttons.

issues: Live market feed shows fake/dummy items (Mythic Immortal $890 / Conqueror S28 $1450 / Gold Butterfly $620) even though marketplace is empty and user asked NOT to seed demo listings — likely hardcoded UI content; consider wiring to real listings or removing.
When a post has no images, main image area shows a game-slug placeholder (e.g. 'ML') with no thumbnail rail — verify intended behavior.

action_items: Investigate custom cursor overlay pointer-events interfering with the Authenticate button click (add pointer-events: none to the cursor element, or ensure it does not sit above form buttons). This affects real users too if the overlay eats pointerdown.
Confirm hero 'Live Market Feed' cards are static UI mock — either wire them to /api/posts (top 3) or remove per the 'no seed listings' requirement.
Optional: add data-testid="marketplace-empty" so empty-state can be asserted (currently marketplace-grid is not rendered when list is empty).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: login lowercases only for exact match then does a case-insensitive regex fallback — consider normalising usernames on write (all lowercase) to avoid the two-step lookup.

server.py: /api/upload sets Authorization requirement via Depends(get_current_user) — good; but the frontend uploadImage() sends undefined Authorization header when no token exists — will still 401 correctly.
server.py: CORS allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False is fine for JWT-in-header; if cookies are added later, tighten origins.

server.py: analytics timeline uses ISO string comparison for date range — works because ISO 8601 sorts lexicographically, but note timezone-aware ISO with '+00:00' suffix; consistent because we store the same format.
AdminLoginPage.jsx: no client-side rate limiting/lockout; brute-force protection relies on backend which currently has none.
PostForm publish path fine; consider disabling Publish while POST is in flight to avoid double-submit.
test_credentials: admin / admin25 (from /app/backend/.env). No /app/memory/test_credentials.md file exists — flagged.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 UI test post 'UITest Post ML' via admin UI (mobile-legends, $77.5, 300K-900K, phone+fb filled). Left in DB for main agent visibility; also created and cleaned up 'TEST_todelete' via pytest fixture.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers auth, games, posts CRUD, upload, analytics, RBAC — 22 tests, all pass. Frontend admin login submit is best triggered via keyboard Enter on the password field rather than clicking the button, until the custom-cursor overlay pointer-events issue is resolved. All other admin actions (New Listing, Publish, etc.) work with page.click(..., force=True). Public URL: https://nexus-gaming-hub-16.preview.emergentagent.com. Test post left in marketplace: 'UITest Post ML'.
rca of the issue: AdminLoginPage submit click flakiness: page has a custom cursor overlay (body cursor:none + a large positioned div). If that overlay div does not have pointer-events:none, the browser dispatches pointerdown/mouseup on the overlay instead of the button, so React's onSubmit never fires. force=True bypasses Playwright's actionability checks but the browser still routes the event to the topmost element at that coordinate. Fix: ensure the cursor element has pointer-events:none in CSS.
